L4-L5 combined-frame premium = -0.36 bp NEGATIVE — train-only LL is marginally better. Total LL lift over raw +2.18-2.55 bp within fold_std 0.00058 noise. Hypothesis falsified: the GBDT extracts lead/lag information implicitly via existing TyreLife/Stint/LapNumber interactions. Combined-frame premium is null. PI sealed prediction (0 bp) vindicated.

metric_check_note: log-loss objective + AUC metric, properly aligned
note: PI sealed prediction: 0 bp (default carryover from 3-for-3 vindication today; will update if PI explicitly revises). Field-state aggregates on (Race,Year,LapNumber) and (Race,Year,LapNumber,Compound) computed from train+test combined. PitStop is a feature column not the label; AV-AUC=0.502 ⇒ Rule 25 PASS. Mechanism: GBDT cannot reconstruct n_drivers_pitting / cum_pits / field-mean-TyreLife from a single row's features; F1 strategy is partly herd-conditional (undercut/overcut). Distinct from probe 1 (own-row sequence shifts) and from Day-16 cohort routing (synth-vs-orig density).

name: probe_field_state
note: Diagnostic-only; no submit. F2 raw 0.94074, F3 raw+combined-frame field-state 0.94230 (+15.58 bp), F4 raw+train-only field-state 0.94241 (+16.66 bp). Combined-frame premium NULL/slightly negative -1.08 bp; the FIELD-STATE MECHANISM ITSELF lifts +15-17 bp standalone. Top single-feat: fs_cum_pits AUC 0.7972 (highest single-feat on comp; raw TyreLife alone is 0.699). fs_pit_rate_now 0.729, fs_compound_pit_rate 0.724. PI sealed prediction 0 bp LB Δ technically vindicated (no submit) but agent OOF prediction +0.5 bp also wrong; this is a 30x OOF lift over predicted band. Structural insight: K=23 pool missing field-state aggregates entirely. Next-step: (a) add to v4 yekenot recipe, retrain CB-v4-fs; (b) standalone field-state-LGBM as K=24 base candidate.

name: probe_field_state
note: K=24 STACK-ADD GATE FAILED. K=23 LR-meta (v4+h1d) OOF 0.95414, K=24 LR-meta (+ field-state) OOF 0.95414, marginal -0.015 bp NULL. field-state |w|=0.0807 vs v4 0.55 / h1d 0.48 — heavily rank-locked. 6th cross-confirmation of lr-meta-rank-lock-strong-anchor friction. Standalone +13.35 bp evaporates at meta layer despite ρ-decoupled features (cross-row aggregates GBDT can't reconstruct). v4's yekenot recipe + h1d's CV TE absorb most of the field-state signal indirectly via Race×Compound×LapNumber × KBins(RaceProgress,200). DO NOT SUBMIT. Right next move: integrate field-state INTO v4 yekenot recipe via Kaggle GPU retrain (~35 min) — bypasses rank-lock by changing the anchor itself, not stacking on top.

Pre-submit-diff vs main PRIMARY (LB 0.95354): ρ=0.999023 borderline tie, top-1% flips 129/154 R7 OK. Per probe.py band ρ in [0.999,0.99996] → LB Δ ≈ OOF Δ - 0.5 = +1.2 bp central. PI authorized.

metric_check_note: hinge / squared-hinge / log-loss are smooth max-margin / log-likelihood proxies; they rank well empirically (LR-bank uses log-loss too). Q6 PASS by analogy. Kernel-logistic variant (Nystroem+LR) is the calibrated cross-check per PI request.
note: First SVM-class entry on this comp (mechanism ledger empty for SVM). Three variants: RFF+SGD-hinge (cheap), Nystroem+LinearSVC (canonical), Nystroem+LR (calibrated). Pricing precedent: LR-bank ceiling 0.928, K=21+1 single-base median ~0bp / non-recipe-transfer best +0.5-1bp. Family is rank-locked — escape requires structural disagreement with GBDT in margin regions.

metric_check_note: smoke phase only; standalone OOF probe to gate full 5-fold spend
note: Smoke gate for SVM family. Cost = 1-fold @ 50k rows × 3 variants. If smoke OOF clears LR-ceiling 0.928, escalate to full 5-fold. If not, family is closed.

name: svm_kernel_smoke_1e
note: Falsified at gate. linsvc K=27+1 Δ=-0.02bp / K=10+1 Δ=-0.09bp; klogreg K=27+1 Δ=-0.00bp / K=10+1 Δ=-0.06bp. Standalone OOF -400bp from PRIMARY. 8th rank-lock confirmation: kernel-class structural diversity (ρ=0.82-0.83) insufficient when standalone AUC gap to GBDT-class is too large.

Single-LGBM specialist on 18737 rain rows (INTERMEDIATE 17382 + WET 1355) gets OOF 0.92641 vs PRIMARY 0.94157 on same rows (-151.53 bp). Mixed prediction (rain=spec, dry=PRIMARY) global OOF 0.95390 vs PRIMARY 0.95432 (-4.17 bp). PRIMARY's 27-base stack extracts cross-Compound transfer signal that a rain-only specialist throws away. A19 inference (27 bases saturate the meta) re-confirmed at the segment level. Logs assumption A22 (rain-specialist axis closed) and refines A5 (residual is INTRINSIC). Open variant: rain-row sample-weighting in a global model preserves transfer; distinct from AV-sample-weighting (bounded null).

Single LGBM full-data with rain-row sample weights w in {1,3,5,10}. Even w=1 (no upweighting) gets rain AUC 0.93455 vs PRIMARY 0.94157 (-70 bp); higher weights hurt more (w=10: -102 bp). K=2 LR-meta [PRIMARY, weighted_LGBM] is uniformly null at -0.14 to -0.17 bp. PRIMARY's 27-base stack already extracts more on rain than a single LGBM can, regardless of class reweighting. A9 rank-lock confirmed at K=2 (smallest meta scale). Refines W1: rain residual is intrinsic given any single-model addition.

Compound × Stint × Year (3-way) on K=27 pool. 120 cells; 43/120 fit at MIN_ROWS=1000 (rest fall back to global). tau in {5k, 20k, 100k}: -2.25, -0.61, -0.16 bp vs PRIMARY (Compound × Stint, tau=100k). At tau=100k all alt segmentations land at near-PRIMARY OOF because heavy shrinkage to global dominates. ρ=0.99942. Combined with d14 sweep (Year/YxStint/Race × tau, 9 variants null at K=21), d18 Compound×Year (null at K=24), Yao/Vehtari covariance variant (null), per-segment-stacker family is now 11+ variants tested all at-or-below PRIMARY. Logs A9b (the rank-lock now extends to hier-meta segmentation crosses).

Compound × RaceProgress-bin (5x5 = 25 cells) on K=27. 21/25 populated. tau in {5k, 20k, 100k}: -1.71, -0.65, -0.16 bp vs PRIMARY. ρ=0.99948. Same conclusion as cs_y — alt segmentations converge to PRIMARY OOF at high tau (heavy shrinkage), and at low tau cells are too small to fit. Path-B amp at K=27 is +0.03 bp vs global LR-meta — exhausted.

K=10 forward-greedy sparse pool + Path-B Compound x Stint tau=100k. Bases: d17_h1d_yekenot_full, p1_single_cb_v4_gpu, f1_hgbc_deep, d16_orig_continuous_only, b_lapsuntilpit, baseline_two_anchor, d9_R6_next_compound, cb_year-cat, e5_optuna_lgbm, d9f_FM_A. PRIMARY (K=27) buys us EXACTLY 1.2 bp on LB, matching the OOF gap to 0.1 bp precision. Confirms (A26) the bank is highly redundant at LB level - 17 of 27 bases produce 1.2 bp of LB lift collectively. Confirms (A27) OOF->LB relative transfer is precise. Falsifies user's hope that sparse pool would break the ceiling - sparse predicts at the ceiling.

GRU at K=10+1 plain LR-meta: -0.045 bp NULL (vs original -0.043 bp at K=22+1). Confirms rank-lock at LR meta is pool-size-independent. Falsifies the 'dense pool was hiding signal' framing for the GRU candidate. Strong prior to skip rerunning field-state / H9 / combined-frame lead/lag - they would absorb identically. The 4th direction must come from a structurally different inductive bias (different task framing), not from re-evaluating prior NULL bases. Logs A28.

K=4 forward-greedy (yekenot-RealMLP + p1_cb_v4 + f1_hgbc_deep + d16_orig_continuous_only) + Path-B C x Stint tau=100k. LB 0.95351 vs PRIMARY 0.95368 (Δ -1.7 bp). OOF Δ -2.93 bp - LB Δ -1.7 bp = LB *better* than OOF predicted by ~1.2 bp. Refines A27: cross-submission LB delta trust band is ~±1 bp, not ±0.1 bp. Logs A26b. K=4 captures 99% of bank's LB value with 15% of bases - 23 of 27 bases buy 1.7 bp on LB collectively. Operationally we can run with 4 bases in production with negligible LB cost.

Three structurally-different inductive biases on 14 raw cols all absorb at K=10+1 LR meta within +/-0.05 bp despite low Spearman rho to K=10 (0.41-0.73). EXP-2 LambdaRank (rho 0.73, Δ +0.042 bp), EXP-3 inter-stint memory (rho 0.47, Δ -0.011), EXP-4 dual-head decomposition (rho 0.41, Δ -0.014 head A / +0.035 composed). Pins down rank-lock mechanism: it's at the LOGIT-direction level, not rank-correlation. K=10 [P, rank, logit] expansion (30 features) reconstructs any new base's logit as linear combination. Different rank info doesn't add logit dimension. Logs A29, A30. Strategic implication: 3-D logit subspace is information ceiling of 14-col row-level data under LR-meta family. Breaking it requires either new data outside 14 cols (PI ruled out external) or non-LR meta architecture (gradient-boosting on predictions or neural meta-learner) - last untested avenue.

Four-variant PCA probe on K=27 ensemble. Variant A (PCA-truncate + LR) shows top-3 PCs score only 0.95061 (-35.64 bp vs K=10 anchor) - the 3.23 variance-eff-rank does NOT predict 3-D predictive ceiling; predictive content spreads across ~15 PCs. Variant B (LightGBM as meta) NULL/NEGATIVE at every input representation: top-K PCs (-1.91 to -32.88), K=10 raw expansion (-1.25), K=27 raw expansion (+0.01); CONFIRMS A30 prediction in the negative direction - non-LR meta is *worse*, not better. EXP-NEW (only architecturally untested avenue per A30) closes FALSIFIED. Variant C (Path-B C×S on PCs) NULL by huge margin (-28 to -34 bp): PCA decorrelates routing variables, killing Path-B's per-segment lift mechanism - clean falsification of the 'Path-B exploits correlation' intuition. Variant D (K=10 LR + K=27 residuals after stripping top-S PCs) recovers K=27+1 plain LR exactly (0.95428) for both S=3 and S=5: the +1 bp K=27→K=27 over K=10 lives in low-variance PCs but the LR meta on K=27 already captures it. No LB submission candidate. Strategic implications: A25 wording wants 'variance' qualifier (predictive eff-rank is closer to 15); A30 'non-LR meta could break ceiling' clause is empirically refuted; only remaining 'new direction' axis per A30 is data outside 14 cols (PI ruled out). Strengthens acceptance/wrap-up posture.
IEEE-CIS Fraud 1st-place trick: groupby(UID).transform('mean') as post-process. UID = (Driver, Race, Year). Pure post-process; no FE pipeline.

name: a3_7_uid_smoothing
note: FAIL. Groupby-mean removes the within-UID lap-to-lap variance that IS the pit-decision signal. Mechanism mismatch with IEEE Fraud (recurring-transaction UID); F1-pit rows within (Driver,Race,Year) have heterogeneous pit probabilities. Tested 4 variants (naive_pure/blend, loo_pure/blend); all regress -124 to -1709 bp. See scripts/artifacts/probe_a3_7_uid_smoothing_results.json.

name: a2_2_mandatory_compound_rule_pathb
note: Path-B amp test on K=4+A2-2 PRIMARY meta. Single-LGBM +1.4 bp absorbed mostly: K=4+1 plain LR-meta +0.30 bp; K=4+1 Path-B C×S τ=100k +0.26 bp. Path-B amp unchanged: +0.34 bp on K=4 vs +0.30 bp on K=4+1. ρ=0.999893 vs PRIMARY (TIE_EXPECTED on pre-submit diff). WEAK verdict; below +0.5 strict gate. Confirms rank-lock prediction at K=4 with Path-B. See scripts/artifacts/probe_a2_2_pathb_K4.json.

name: a2_8_stack_meta_K4
note: LightGBM stack-meta on K=4: 43 meta features (4 raw P + 4 logits + 4 ranks + 6 pairwise products + 6 abs-diffs + 6 logit-diffs + 8 numeric side-info + 5 Compound one-hot). Hyper: depth 4, leaves 31, lr 0.05, l2 1.0, bag 0.9, ff 0.9, min_data 200. 5-fold OOF 0.95390 vs Path-B PRIMARY 0.95403 (-1.30 bp); also below plain LR 0.95399 (-0.96 bp). Fold-std 0.00080 (elevated; range 0.95309-0.95506). Confirms prior EXP-NEW falsification: tree stackers overfit interaction noise on small-K pools; convex LR + Path-B partial-pooling regularize better. See scripts/artifacts/probe_a2_8_stack_meta.json.

metric_check_note: Aggregate features added to LR meta-stacker on K=4 [P, rank, logit]; same row-level binary cross-entropy objective as PRIMARY; metric is row-AUC; no target reframing.
note: Bypasses the 1.4% driver-row-match cap by joining at (Race, Year, LapNumber) — Race + Year preserved through synth. Targets W1 rain-residual (WET ECE 11x other compounds) + A13b live-low-confidence. Precedent: d16 orig continuous_only +3.33 bp K=21+1 (best base-add of session); d17 h2 hard-join closed null at 1.4% match — DIFFERENT mechanism (aggregates vs row-join). Prior calibrated cautious because 8 rank-lock confirmations.

metric_check_note: Per-segment LGBM head replaces inner LR in Path-B; outer combiner remains LR; meta input is K=4 [P, rank, logit]; objective is binary AUC at row level — no reframing.
note: W5 third bullet (untested non-LR per-segment head) ; Path-B has TWO LR layers, we falsified outer (non-LR meta) but never inner (per-segment LGBM); 11+ per-segment-stacker variants all LR-class so far. P(useful) 0.20 reflects 11/11 prior nulls on segment-axis variants but B is structurally novel (tree per segment exploits non-linear interactions inside Compound x Stint cells).

metric_check_note: Transformer outputs PitNextLap probability per row via sigmoid; binary cross-entropy training; row-AUC metric — aligned.
note: EXP-9 in EXPERIMENTS-NEXT.md; d16 GRU was NULL but had no gap modelling; A32 says gap-as-feature is absorbed but gap-as-positional-encoding is architecturally different. Cost 60 min CPU author+push (Kaggle GPU runs async, no local CPU spent). P(useful) 0.18 reflects A32 narrowing.

metric_check_note: Layer-1 stackers (LGBM/XGB/CB/MLP) all output binary-AUC probabilities; Layer-2 LR over (base + L1) OOFs is binary cross-entropy; metric is row-AUC; full alignment.
note: Replicates masayakawamata-stack architecture (LB 0.9544 from 2 base OOFs via cuML 2-layer stack). We've tested 11 per-segment-stacker variants (all LR-class L2) and 2 non-LR L2 (falsified). NEVER tested L1 STACKERS as a separate layer feeding L2 LR. Direct architectural gap. Precedent: masaya 2-base 2-layer at LB 0.9544 (+9 bp from us); ours has 4 bases so absorption ceiling argument may not bite. P(useful) 0.40 reflects the empirical existence proof on the public LB.

metric_check_note: Per-segment LGBM head replaces inner LR in Path-B; outer combiner remains LR; meta input is K=4 [P, rank, logit]; binary cross-entropy with row-AUC metric.
note: W5 third bullet untested (non-LR per-segment head in Path-B). 11 prior per-segment variants all LR-class. Cheap CPU-only falsification. P(useful) 0.20 reflects 11/11 prior LR-class nulls but B is structurally novel (tree per-segment exploits non-linear interactions). Cost 90 min revised down from 120 (parallel with Prong S).

name: seq_coupled_v1_lr_full
note: V1.2 LR meta on K=4 [P,rank,logit]+24 seq-coupled features (36 feat) 5-fold OOF AUC 0.95401 vs LR row-local 12-feat 0.95400 (+0.03 bp WEAK). Diagnostic confirmed the 24 seq-coupled features have R²=0.487 vs row-local span — 51% non-overlap variance — yet LR meta extracts +0.03 bp. Refines A29 framing: rank-lock is at the conditional-target-correlation level, not just the logit-direction level. See scripts/seq_coupled/diag_lookahead_orthogonality.py + fit_meta.py + audit/2026-05-08-seq-coupled-meta-probe.md.

name: seq_coupled_v2_interactions_residual_lgbm
note: V2 sharpenings on 36-feat input. V2.1 LR + hand interactions (47 feat): OOF 0.95400 (-0.30 bp vs PRIMARY) TIE. V2.2 stage-1 LR row-local + stage-2 Ridge on residuals: OOF 0.95369 (-3.43 bp REGRESS) — Ridge fits noise. V2.3 LightGBM meta on 36-feat: OOF 0.95397 (-0.59 bp REGRESS). Confirms tree-stack-meta-overfits-small-K-pool friction even with sequence-coupled feature enrichment. No conditional structure breaks the rank-lock at K=4.

name: seq_coupled_v1_ranknet
note: V1.3 RankNet pairwise meta on 36-feat (K=4 row-local + 24 seq-coupled): 5-fold OOF AUC 0.95399 vs LR row-local 0.95400 (-0.12 bp). RankNet uses direct AUC objective via pairwise sigmoid loss on 2M pos/neg pairs/fold × 200 epochs × momentum SGD. Per-fold AUCs match LR's row-local within <0.1 bp on every fold. The ceiling is information, not optimisation. Refines structural finding: enriching meta input with sequence-coupled features adds +0.03 bp under cross-entropy and -0.12 bp under direct AUC; loss-function variation does not escape the conditional-target-correlation ceiling at K=4. probe_seq_coupled_meta.json final summary.

name: seq_coupled_v3_knn_meta
note: V3 LR meta on K=4 [P,rank,logit] + kNN target-mean + discrepancy (14 feat). 5-fold OOF AUC 0.95401 vs LR row-local 0.95400 (+0.01 bp). Per-fold: 0.95496/0.95311/0.95371/0.95339/0.95503 — all within ±0.03 bp of LR row-local. Bare y_knn AUC 0.8979 standalone (substantial signal!), discrepancy correlation with y = -0.357 (real). YET the LR meta extracts +0.01 bp from these features. Confirms K=4 conditional-target-correlation ceiling EVEN under transductive label-derived features at the meta layer. The path forward narrows to (a) external data, (b) genuinely new base class with new source information. V4 (kNN as base input) tests whether non-linear tree ingestion lifts where linear meta absorption blocks.

name: seq_coupled_v4_knn_aug_base
note: V4 kNN-augmented LightGBM base: 5-fold OOF standalone AUC 0.94163. K=4+1 plain LR-meta gate +0.24 bp on OOF (5/5 folds positive, range +0.12 to +0.44 bp). FIRST base in 8 days to produce a consistently positive K=4+1 lift, but BELOW +0.5 bp PASS gate (WEAK verdict). ρ_spearman vs K=4 bases 0.85-0.94, most diverse vs d16_orig (0.85). Tree-split ingestion of kNN-target-mean extracts +0.24 bp at meta, where the same feature at META layer in V3 extracted only +0.01 bp — confirms tree non-linearity DOES retrieve some signal LR misses, but magnitude is small.

name: seq_coupled_K5_path_b
note: Path-B Compound×Stint τ=100k production gate. K=4 PRIMARY OOF re-derived at 0.95403 (matches A1). K=5 (K=4+V4) Path-B OOF 0.95405 (Δ +0.20 bp). ρ_test_K5_vs_K4 = 0.99989 — exceeds Rule 27 abort threshold (0.999). LB expected to tie. ABORT submission per Rule 27. Submission file built but NOT pushed: submissions/submission_K5_kNNaugbase_pathb.csv. Held for potential R5 hedge in final-window if PI reviews.

name: submit_K5_kNNaugbase_pathb
note: K=5 (K=4 + V4 kNN-augmented base) + Path-B C×S τ=100k. Public LB 0.95359 vs K=4 PRIMARY 0.95351 = +0.8 bp. OOF was +0.20 bp; LB amplification ~4x (partly sample noise; partly real V4 contribution). Rule 27 abort guidance (ρ_test 0.99989 > 0.999 => tie) was empirically wrong here; LB moved +0.8 bp. Calibration: ρ in 0.999-0.9999 zone can still produce sub-bp to few-bp LB movement, not auto-tie. K=27 d18 PRIMARY (0.95368) remains LB best by 9 bp; V4 didn't bridge that gap. Submitted by PI explicit Rule 27 override 2026-05-09.
